articles.title: Saudi-led coalition pledges ‘unprecedented’ force against Houthi threats
articles.summary: Threats escalate after Houthis block Saudi warplanes, allowing Iranian plane to land in Sanaa for first time in decade.
articles.titleZh: 沙特领导的联军承诺以“前所未有”的武力应对胡塞武装的威胁
articles.summaryZh: 胡塞武装拦截沙特战机，允许伊朗飞机十年来首次降落在萨那，威胁升级。

contentParagraphs.en: Threats escalate after Houthis block Saudi warplanes, allowing Iranian aircraft to land in Sanaa for first time in about a decade.The Saudi-led coalition supporting Yemen’s internationally recognised government says it will respond with “unprecedented determination and force” to any attempt to target the kingdom or violate Yemeni sovereignty.This followed earlier threats from the Houthi movement and the arrival of an Iranian civilian flight in Yemen’s Houthi-controlled capital, Sanaa.Recommended Stories list of 3 itemslist 1 of 3Yemen’s Houthis threaten Saudi Arabia after alleged airspace intrusionlist 2 of 3Yemen’s Houthis declare ban on Israeli ships sailing the Red Sealist 3 of 3For one Yemeni, World Cup serves as a marker of war and peaceend of listIn a statement released on Saturday, coalition spokesperson Major-General Turki al-Maliki dismissed recent Houthi threats against Saudi Arabia as an attempt to divert attention from the group’s actions against the Yemeni people.He accused the Iran-aligned movement of seeking to export economic hardships it has caused and to deflect from domestic political and social challenges.“The claims they made are an extension of escalations and hostile behaviour demonstrated by the Houthi Militia and their attempts to undermine regional and international security,” al-Maliki said.Houthi threatsOn Friday, Houthi military spokesperson Yahya Saree had issued a statement threatening a “comprehensive” response targeting Saudi airports and vital interests on land and sea.Saree said his forces had used air defence missiles to prevent Saudi warplanes from blocking an Iranian civilian aircraft from landing at Sanaa International Airport.He said the plane was carrying more than 200 patients alongside a Houthi delegation travelling to Tehran for the funeral of Iran’s late supreme leader.The flight was the first publicly confirmed Iranian civilian aircraft to land in Sanaa in roughly a decade.Al-Maliki’s statement on Saturday said the Houthis’ military posture has exposed Yemeni civilian infrastructure to potential targeting, including the ports of Hodeidah, Ras Isa, and as-Salif, as well as Sanaa International Airport, power stations and industrial facilities.“The coalition will respond with unprecedented determination and force to any and all attempts to target the Kingdom, its citizens and residents and national assets, or any attempt to violate the sovereignty of the brotherly Republic of Yemen,” al-Maliki said.The coalition also reiterated previous accusations that the Houthis have attacked shipping lanes and international trade in the southern Red Sea and Bab al-Mandeb Strait.CondemnationsYemen’s Presidential Leadership Council, which is recognised internationally, held an emergency meeting on Friday, chaired by President Rashad al-Alimi.In a statement, the council condemned the Iranian flight as a violation of Yemeni sovereignty and said it defied international law and United Nations Security Council resolutions.It warned Tehran against further escalation and called on the UN and regional partners to take what it described as deterrent measures, including tighter controls on channels supporting and arming the Houthis.The Saudi-led coalition intervened in Yemen in 2015 after the Houthis seized Sanaa and ousted the internationally backed government. The conflict has since caused widespread displacement and damage, with the UN describing the situation as one of the world’s most severe humanitarian crises.
contentParagraphs.zh: 胡塞武装拦截沙特战机，允许伊朗飞机在大约十年来首次降落在萨那。沙特领导的支持也门国际公认政府的联盟表示，将以“前所未有的决心和武力”回应任何针对该王国或侵犯也门主权的企图。在此之前，胡塞运动曾发出威胁，一架伊朗民用飞机抵达也门胡塞控制的首都萨那。推荐故事清单（共 3 项） 1 of 3 也门胡塞武装在涉嫌领空入侵后威胁沙特阿拉伯list 2 of 3也门胡塞武装宣布禁止以色列船只航行红海利士号 3 of 3对于也门人来说，世界杯是战争与和平的标志end of list周六发布的一份声明中，联军发言人图尔基·马利基少将驳斥了胡塞武装最近对沙特阿拉伯的威胁，称其为试图转移人们对该组织针对也门行动的注意力他指责与伊朗结盟的运动试图出口其造成的经济困难，并转移对国内政治和社会挑战的关注。马利基说：“他们的说法是胡塞民兵所表现出的升级和敌对行为的延伸，以及他们企图破坏地区和国际安全的行为。”萨雷表示，他的部队使用防空导弹阻止沙特战机拦截一架伊朗民用飞机降落在萨那国际机场。他说，这架飞机载有 200 多名患者，与胡塞武装代表团一起前往德黑兰参加伊朗已故最高领导人的葬礼。这架航班是大约十年来第一架公开证实降落在萨那的伊朗民用飞机。马利基周六发表声明称，胡塞武装的军事态势使也门民用基础设施面临潜在目标，包括荷台达、拉斯伊萨和萨利夫的港口，以及萨那国际机场、发电站和工业设施。马利基说：“联军将以前所未有的决心和武力，回应任何针对也门王国、其公民和居民以及国家资产的企图，或任何侵犯兄弟的也门共和国主权的企图。”联军还重申了之前的指控，即胡塞武装袭击了红海南部的航道和国际贸易，曼德海峡。谴责国际公认的也门总统领导委员会周五举行紧急会议，由总统拉沙德·阿里米主持。该委员会在一份声明中谴责伊朗航班侵犯了也门主权，并表示这违反了国际法和联合国安理会决议。它警告德黑兰不要进一步升级，并呼吁联合国和区域伙伴采取所谓的威慑措施，包括对渠道进行更严格的控制支持和武装胡塞武装。2015年，胡塞武装占领萨那并推翻国际支持的政府后，沙特领导的联军对也门进行了干预。此后，这场冲突造成了广泛的流离失所和破坏，联合国将这种局势描述为世界上最严重的人道主义危机之一。

articles.title: A hot summer trend in the sharing economy? Rental swimming pools
articles.summary: The Airbnb-style company Swimply said there have been about 275,000 private pool reservations so far this year.
articles.titleZh: 共享经济夏季火热趋势？出租游泳池
articles.summaryZh: Airbnb 风格的公司 Swimply 表示，今年迄今为止已有约 275,000 个私人泳池预订。

contentParagraphs.en: The Endless Summer Oasis pool listing on Swimply gets more than 1,000 guests a season, according to owner Jasmine Lawson in Birmingham, Ala.
contentParagraphs.zh: 据阿拉巴马州伯明翰的业主 Jasmine Lawson 称，Swimply 上的 Endless Summer Oasis 泳池每个季度都会接待超过 1,000 名客人。

contentParagraphs.en: Sign up for NPR Life Kit newsletter for expert advice on lifestyle topics like health, money, relationships and more.
contentParagraphs.zh: 订阅 NPR Life Kit 时事通讯，获取有关健康、金钱、人际关系等生活方式主题的专家建议。

contentParagraphs.en: A scorching Alabama day isn't so bad — if there's a pool party. Meghan Clopton invited dozens of guests to a summer birthday celebration for one of her kids last year, complete with water guns, a twisting slide and plenty of inflatables.
contentParagraphs.zh: 阿拉巴马州的炎热天气还不错——如果有泳池派对的话。去年，梅根·克洛普顿 (Meghan Clopton) 邀请了数十位客人参加她的一个孩子的夏季生日庆祝活动，并配备了水枪、旋转滑梯和大量充气玩具。

contentParagraphs.en: The guests had just one shared question: Whose pool was this?
contentParagraphs.zh: 客人们只有一个共同的问题：这是谁的游泳池？

contentParagraphs.en: Her answer? She rented it.
contentParagraphs.zh: 她的答案？她租的。

contentParagraphs.en: "It's part of the culture now, right? Just, like, take over someone else's house or pool for the day or the weekend," Clopton said.
contentParagraphs.zh: “现在这已经成为文化的一部分，对吧？就像，在白天或周末接管别人的房子或游泳池，”克洛普顿说。

contentParagraphs.en: "For a fee," her husband, Taylor Clopton, added.
contentParagraphs.zh: “要收费，”她的丈夫泰勒·克洛普顿补充道。

contentParagraphs.en: They rented the backyard pool through Swimply, one of a small but growing number of Airbnb-style companies that lets you rent pools, along with other private spaces, including tennis and basketball courts. These listings are a growing side of the sharing economy that have made it common, and often near-frictionless, to rent someone else's property, from cars to guest bedrooms.
contentParagraphs.zh: 他们通过 Swimply 租用了后院游泳池，Swimply 是少数但数量不断增加的 Airbnb 风格公司之一，允许您租用游泳池以及其他私人空间，包括网球场和篮球场。这些房源是共享经济不断增长的一个方面，使得租用别人的房产（从汽车到客房）变得很常见，而且往往几乎没有摩擦。

contentParagraphs.en: Clopton paid $381 for that birthday party, which allowed her to invite up to 30 guests for three hours of pool time.
contentParagraphs.zh: Clopton 为那场生日派对支付了 381 美元，这样她就可以邀请最多 30 位客人参加三个小时的泳池活动。

contentParagraphs.en: The pool's owner, Jasmine Lawson, said she's had bookings for graduation parties, book clubs and photo shoots. Overall, she's hosted over 1,000 guests a year at her Birmingham property. "And it grows every single year," Lawson added.
contentParagraphs.zh: 泳池的主人贾斯敏·劳森 (Jasmine Lawson) 表示，她已经预订了毕业派对、读书俱乐部和摄影活动。总体而言，她在伯明翰的酒店每年接待超过 1,000 名客人。 “而且它每年都在增长，”劳森补充道。

contentParagraphs.en: Along with a hot tub and an 8-foot-deep pool, Lawson's guests get access to an air-conditioned room in her home with a table for laying out a party spread, plus a private bathroom. They also get to choose from a catalog of 50 different pool floats. (The white, human-sized inflatable unicorn that sprays water from its horn is a favorite.)
contentParagraphs.zh: 除了热水浴缸和 8 英尺深的游泳池外，劳森的客人还可以使用她家中的空调房间，房内配有一张用于布置派对的桌子和一间私人浴室。他们还可以从 50 种不同的泳池浮标中进行选择。 （人们最喜欢的是白色的、人形大小的充气独角兽，它能用角喷水。）

contentParagraphs.en: A unicorn inflatable sprinkler sprays water across Jasmine Lawson's pool in Birmingham, Ala., on June 24.
contentParagraphs.zh: 6 月 24 日，阿拉巴马州伯明翰，独角兽充气喷水器向 Jasmine Lawson 的泳池喷水。

contentParagraphs.en: Lawson gives all her guests a walk-through when they arrive, before going upstairs to work. "But if they ever need anything, I'm right down here helping them as soon as I can," she said. Swimply users can use a filter on the platform for more privacy — that can include factors like whether or not the pool is within view of the home or if the owners will be around.
contentParagraphs.zh: 当所有客人到达后，劳森都会在他们上楼上班之前先对他们进行一次巡查。 “但如果他们需要任何东西，我就在这里尽快帮助他们，”她说。 Swimly 用户可以使用平台上的过滤器来获得更多隐私，其中可以包括游泳池是否在房屋视野范围内或业主是否在附近等因素。

contentParagraphs.en: Lawson originally started renting out her pool to help cover the end-of-life veterinary costs for one of her dogs, a Cavalier King Charles spaniel. Now, Lawson uses the bookings to boost her income and cover the pool's maintenance.
contentParagraphs.zh: 劳森最初开始出租她的游泳池，以帮助支付她的一只狗——查理王小猎犬的临终兽医费用。现在，劳森利用这些预订来增加收入并支付泳池的维护费用。

contentParagraphs.en: "When you own a pool, every time you turn around something happens and it's $1,000," Lawson said.
contentParagraphs.zh: “当你拥有一个泳池时，每次转身都会发生一些事情，并且损失 1,000 美元，”劳森说。

contentParagraphs.en: Swimply founder and CEO Bunim Laskin said covering those expenses was the original idea for the company. He started Swimply in 2019, inspired by his experience of offering to pay a pool-owning neighbor to borrow it for a party with his 11 siblings.
contentParagraphs.zh: Swimply 创始人兼首席执行官 Bunim Laskin 表示，承担这些费用是该公司的最初想法。他于 2019 年创立了 Swimply，灵感来自于他向拥有泳池的邻居付钱借用泳池与他的 11 个兄弟姐妹一起举办派对的经历。

contentParagraphs.en: Today, according to a company spokesperson, Swimply has listings in 150 cities, and so far has had more than 275,000 reservations this year, about 50% more than last year.
contentParagraphs.zh: 如今，据公司发言人透露，Swimply 已在 150 个城市上市，今年迄今已有超过 27.5 万预订量，比去年增加约 50%。

contentParagraphs.en: While homes listed on Airbnb, the best-known sharing platform, are often used by out-of-towners, such as for business trips or vacations, Swimply's rental pools are more often used locally. Guests book pools near home, often for staycations.
contentParagraphs.zh: 虽然最著名的共享平台 Airbnb 上列出的房屋通常由外地人使用，例如商务旅行或度假，但 Swimply 的租赁池更多时候是本地人使用。客人会预订家附近的泳池，通常是为了居家度假。

contentParagraphs.en: Laskin said the company has done well during tough economic times. "We really became big for the first time during the pandemic," Laskin said. "Travel was impossible, and people more than ever needed a way to supplement their income."
contentParagraphs.zh: 拉斯金表示，该公司在经济困难时期表现良好。 “在大流行期间，我们确实第一次变得强大，”拉斯金说。 “旅行是不可能的，人们比以往任何时候都更需要一种方式来补充他们的收入。”

contentParagraphs.en: Jasmine Lawson skims her pool in Birmingham, Ala., on June 24.
contentParagraphs.zh: 6 月 24 日，贾斯敏·劳森 (Jasmine Lawson) 在阿拉巴马州伯明翰的泳池里游泳。

contentParagraphs.en: Renting out a swimming pool comes with an important, and possibly expensive, question: Who's responsible if someone gets hurt? After all, pools can be dangerous, especially for young swimmers.
contentParagraphs.zh: 出租游泳池会带来一个重要且可能昂贵的问题：如果有人受伤，谁该负责？毕竟，游泳池可能很危险，尤其是对于年轻的游泳者来说。

contentParagraphs.en: Swimply covers up to $1 million in liability for hosts, similar to Airbnb's policy for home rentals.
contentParagraphs.zh: Swimply 为房东承担高达 100 万美元的责任，类似于 Airbnb 的房屋租赁政策。

contentParagraphs.en: Courts have been wrestling with this kind of question when it comes to gig and sharing economy companies, according to Lindsey Cameron, assistant professor at the University of Pennsylvania's Wharton School of Business. For example: Who's liable if a rideshare driver harms a passenger — the driver or the company? "Because there is not an employer, as one typically thinks of, that you can say, 'You have responsibility for the products that you are putting out into the world,'" she said.
contentParagraphs.zh: 宾夕法尼亚大学沃顿商学院助理教授林赛·卡梅伦表示，在涉及零工和共享经济公司时，法院一直在努力解决此类问题。例如：如果共享出行司机伤害了乘客，谁应该承担责任——司机还是公司？ “因为没有一个雇主，正如人们通常认为的那样，可以说，‘你对你向世界推出的产品负责，’”她说。

contentParagraphs.en: Some state governments are trying to hash out these kinds of issues, too. This week, Minnesota's Supreme Court agreed to hear a case about whether pools on the Swimply app should be considered public facilities — and therefore subject to government licensing regulations, possibly including state inspections. (A lower court ruled in favor of the regulations earlier this year.)
contentParagraphs.zh: 一些州政府也在试图解决此类问题。本周，明尼苏达州最高法院同意审理有关 Swimply 应用程序上的游泳池是否应被视为公共设施的案件，因此应遵守政府许可法规，可能包括国家检查。 （下级法院今年早些时候裁定支持该规定。）

contentParagraphs.en: Saša Pekeč, a professor of business administration at Duke University's Fuqua School of Business, compares it to the early days of ridesharing apps, which were banned in some cities while regulations were still being worked out.
contentParagraphs.zh: 杜克大学福卡商学院工商管理教授 Saša Pekeč 将其与早期的拼车应用程序进行了比较，这些应用程序在一些城市被禁止，而法规仍在制定中。

contentParagraphs.en: "Some local communities might just say that 'No, you cannot rent your pool because there's too much liability,'" said Pekeč.
contentParagraphs.zh: “一些当地社区可能会说‘不，你不能租用游泳池，因为责任太多，’”佩克奇说。

contentParagraphs.en: The prospect of a pool being used as a rental has even given at least one private company pause. Lawson said her pool maintenance company dropped her as a client, citing worries that they'd be held responsible if a guest had a bad reaction to pool chemicals. Now she maintains the pool on her own. ("It's been crystal clear," she said.)
contentParagraphs.zh: 泳池被用作出租的前景甚至让至少一家私人公司犹豫不决。劳森说，她的泳池维护公司放弃了她的客户身份，理由是担心如果客人对泳池化学品产生不良反应，他们将承担责任。现在她自己维护这个游泳池。 （“一切都非常清楚，”她说。）

contentParagraphs.en: But other than having to offer the occasional Band-Aid, Lawson said, she's never had an incident. And that's with weekends with three or four bookings back-to-back. This weekend she's got an all-day Fourth of July birthday party booked.
contentParagraphs.zh: 劳森说，除了偶尔需要提供创可贴之外，她从未发生过任何事故。这是周末连续三到四次预订的情况。这个周末她预订了一场全天的国庆节生日派对。

contentParagraphs.en: Meghan and Taylor Clopton stand in front of their backyard on June 1
contentParagraphs.zh: 6 月 1 日，梅根和泰勒·克洛普顿站在自家后院前

articles.title: Tackle workplace sickness to unlock hidden growth, former John Lewis boss says
articles.summary: More than 250 of the UK's biggest employers have signed up to his 'Get Britain Working' taskforce.
articles.titleZh: 前约翰刘易斯老板表示，解决职场疾病可以释放隐藏的增长
articles.summaryZh: 超过 250 家英国最大的雇主已签署加入他的“让英国工作”工作组。

contentParagraphs.en: Tackling unemployment linked to long-term illness will unlock economic growth that's "hiding in plain sight", former John Lewis chair Sir Charlie Mayfield has said.More than 250 of the UK's biggest employers, including British Airways, Tesco, Royal Mail, and several government departments, have signed up to his Get Britain Working taskforce.The group aims to prevent people dropping out of work due to ill-health and encourage those signed off to come back, with official figures showing the issue costs the UK £212bn a year.However, some employers have said previously that tax rises mean many firms cannot afford to invest, while others have warned against pushing ill people into work.The companies signed up will track sickness absence, return-to-work outcomes, and disability participation, which the government said would make workplace health performance visible for the first time.Many big UK businesses, including Sainsbury's, EDF Energy, and Currys, as well as 10 mayoral authorities, including London and Manchester, have agreed to take part.Sir Charlie told the BBC: "I can't tell you how many people I've met who said: 'I was signed off work for three months, or six months, and I never had any contact with my employer at all.' "That's not because the employer is a bad person. It's because we've got a situation at the minute where people don't talk to each other when they really need to."Sir Charlie's comments come as pressure grows on Andy Burnham, who is widely expected to take over as prime minister later this month, to reduce the UK's welfare bill to free up money elsewhere.According to government figures, total welfare spending in Great Britain is forecast to be 23.6% of the total amount the government spends in the 2025 to 2026 financial year.Sir Charlie said his plans could help cut that bill."Fixing these problems at the fundamental level, could make a really big contribution to getting this economy working better — for employers, for employees, for the taxpayer, for all of us."He added: "This is not a zero-sum game. It's not a question of employers win and employees lose and vice versa. Everybody can win."Sir Charlie suggested Burnham would back his plans."I can't see any reason why he wouldn't because of what Andy has said about good growth. If this isn't good growth, I'm not sure what is, quite frankly."He said getting people back into work who are currently not working due to ill-health would be a simple way of boosting the workforce."You wouldn't have had to build a single house, open a new channel of immigration, you wouldn't have to wait for a cohort of young people to join the workplace. This is basically growth hiding in plain sight."
contentParagraphs.zh: QUERY LENGTH LIMIT EXCEEDED. MAX ALLOWED QUERY : 500 CHARS

articles.title: UK temperatures forecast to exceed 30C as another heatwave imminent
articles.summary: Another heatwave is on its way and set to last significantly longer as Stav Danaos explains.
articles.titleZh: 英国气温预计将超过 30 摄氏度，另一场热浪即将来临
articles.summaryZh: 正如斯塔夫·达纳奥斯（Stav Danaos）解释的那样，另一场热浪即将到来，并将持续更长时间。

contentParagraphs.en: Image source, EPAByStav DanaosLead Weather PresenterParts of the UK are set to heat up from this weekend, with forecasters warning that temperatures could exceed 30C.For some areas this will mark the third heatwave of the year.Highs could reach 33C for southern and south-eastern England with 30C possible as far north as South Yorkshire. It will be cooler in the west. While this latest hot spell is not expected to be as intense as the record-breaking heatwave in late June, it looks set to last significantly longer, potentially into the middle of the month.Image caption, Temperatures are expected to climb across western Europe in the week ahead, with many cities exceeding 30C againHow long will this heatwave last?Temperatures will start to gradually rise over the weekend as high pressure from the Azores builds over the UK.The warmest conditions are expected from Monday, with temperatures across much of England and Wales likely to peak during the middle to latter part of the week.Several areas may meet official heatwave criteria if these temperatures persist for at least three consecutive days.The highest temperatures will be focused across southern and eastern England, where values could exceed 30C widely. In the north-east of England and eastern Scotland temperatures could reach the mid to high 20s.Areas on the western side of the UK - such as Lancashire, the Lake District, western Scotland and Northern Ireland - will be exposed to an Atlantic breeze which will act like a natural air conditioner limiting temperatures to the low to mid-20s. Currently many forecasting models show that the hot spell could persist into the middle of July - although unlikely to reach the record breaking temperatures we saw in June.Heat heath alerts issuedYellow heat health alerts have been issued by the UK Health Security Agency for the East Midlands, West Midlands, east of England, London, south-east England and south-west of England, for between noon on Saturday and 20:00 BST on Saturday 11 July. They warn of a heightened risk to health, particularly for vulnerable people, due to the expected heat.In the UK, a heatwave is defined as a period of at least three consecutive days where daily maximum temperatures meet or exceed regional thresholds. These vary by location, but are typically around 25C in much of the country and 28C in London and parts of the south-east.During such conditions, there is an increased risk of heat-related illness, especially among older people and those with underlying health conditions.What is driving this spell of hot weather?A ridge of high pressure linked to the Azores High - a large semi-permanent centre of high atmospheric pressure - is expected to extend north-eastwards across the UK, while the jet stream shifts further north. This will allow high pressure to dominate, blocking Atlantic low-pressure systems and leading to a more settled weather pattern, bringing plenty of sunshine, dry weather and another notable burst of summer heat for many.Get in touchHow have you been affected by heatwaves? Have you changed your work and living arrangements to cope?
contentParagraphs.zh: QUERY LENGTH LIMIT EXCEEDED. MAX ALLOWED QUERY : 500 CHARS

articles.title: Key takeaways from Round of 32 stage at World Cup 2026
articles.summary: A thrilling Golden Boot race hots up, France in scintillating form, and Messi and Argentina squeak past Cape Verde.
articles.titleZh: 2026 年世界杯 32 强赛阶段的要点
articles.summaryZh: 一场激动人心的金靴争夺战即将拉开帷幕，法国队状态火爆，梅西和阿根廷队则险胜佛得角。

contentParagraphs.en: The World Cup, in case we needed reminding, doesn’t truly get going until the knockout rounds.No making up for an off day. No second chances. Crank up the intensity. Stale Solbakken apparently got it right – save your starters for the second round.Recommended Stories list of 4 itemslist 1 of 4Messi scores again but Argentina given World Cup upset fright by Cape Verdelist 2 of 4England vs Mexico World Cup knockout match to go ahead as scheduledlist 3 of 4Egypt confirms police officer pushed player, director at World Cup hotellist 4 of 4Salah awaits Messi as Egypt beat Australia on penalties at World Cupend of listSeven UEFA teams advanced from the Round of 32, among them Solbakken’s Norway, sufficiently rested after basically conceding Group I to France. Tri-hosts Canada, Mexico and the United States made it three CONCACAF representatives in the Round of 16 for the second time, and the first since 2014 (when it was Costa Rica, Mexico, and the US).Four South American countries got through: Argentina, Brazil, Colombia, and Paraguay.Paraguay’s methods were un-Solbakken – all-out effort to squeeze through the group stage, followed by a quick turnaround and cross-country fight to meet Germany in Foxborough, Massachusetts.But La Albirroja had enough left in the tank to become the first team to ever beat Die Mannschaft on penalties at the World Cup.Les Bleus lead the wayFrance’s front four – Ousmane Dembele, Desire Doue, Kylian Mbappe, and Michael Olise – proved unstoppable in the group stage. Furthermore, Bradley Barcola replaced Doue and upped the pace in a 3-0 victory over Sweden in the last 32. Les Bleus’ only problem could be an abundance of attackers.Can coach Didier Deschamps keep everyone, including Rayan Cherki, happy?Europeans set standardsBelgium, England, Portugal, Spain and Switzerland joined France and Norway as winners in the Round of 32.Belgium and England had to rally against African opposition. The Belgians were 2-0 down against Senegal before Youri Tielemans and Romelu Lukaku sent the match into extra time, with Tielemans’ penalty kick deciding the contest at 3-2.England was tested by DR Congo before Harry Kane’s goals provided a 2-1 win.Portugal edged Croatia 2-1, as we learned a chip in the World Cup ball can register a hair’s breadth touch, which a VAR review used to strike off a Croatian equaliser 15-plus minutes into added time.Demise of traditional World Cup powersAdd Germany to the list of declining World Cup powers, following Italy (who failed to qualify) and Uruguay (out after the group stage).Until recently, Die Mannschaft looked primed to challenge for a fifth title. Julian Nagelsmann, 38, and a mix of veterans – led by 40-year-old goalkeeper Manuel Neuer – and youngsters looked like they would lead a revival. Instead, Germany shockingly went down to Paraguay on penalty kicks, the first time that has happened in the World Cup, and the first penalty kick loss for Germany since the 1976 Euros.Jonathan Tah’s header could have done the trick, but goalkeeper interference was detected by a VAR review.“This is the third elimination in a row, so we are not part of the first-class teams any more,” said Nagelsmann, who has since departed and may be replaced by Jurgen Klopp.Africans leave their markDraws with former champions Spain and Uruguay? Just a warm-up for Cabo Verde, the ultimate underdogs, who took Argentina to extra time before falling, 3-2, due to a 111th-minute Diney Borges own goal.Though Algeria, Ivory Coast, DR Congo, Ghana, Senegal and South Africa all went out in the Round of 32, they made the game worth watching.Maybe a bit of cynical play would have helped. Or some uncompromising defending, which turned out to work for Egypt and Morocco – who knocked out Australia and the Netherlands, respectively.For all the talk of going on the attack under Mohamed Ouahbi, the Atlas Lions have, so far, stuck to their 2022 defensive guns. An injury-time finish from Issa Diop and penalty-kick stonewalling from Yassine Bounou took them past the Netherlands.Golden Boot raceLionel Messi’s surgical touch led to his scoring the opener for Argentina against Cape Verde, upping his goal total to seven.Meanwhile, after playing creator against Norway, Kylian Mbappe returned to scoring mode with two goals against Sweden. That gives Mbappe, seeking to become the first back-to-back Golden Boot winner, six goals.Norway’s Erling Haaland and England’s Harry Kane saved their scoring for late – they each have five.Messi also leads the all-time list, with 20, two more than Mbappe.Home advantage definitely countsAt least that has been true for Mexico and the US.El Tri improved a World Cup unbeaten winning streak that dates to 1970 at Estadio Azteca, going into their last 16 clash with England. Mexico, who have also compiled a 360-minute shutout streak, has made it to the “Quinto Partido” (“Fifth Game”) for the first time since 1986, but the expanded field means it remains a match short of the quarterfinals. The downside: There were at least four celebration-related deaths in Mexico.Before the start of the tournament, there were concerns about the US crowd getting into the spirit. Those have been erased following a 2-0 victory over Bosnia and Herzegovina in Santa Clara, California. The result could be costly, though, as Folarin Balogun will be suspended after receiving a red card, following a VAR review.Canada, meanwhile, became the first cohost to take its act on the road, to California and Texas. The Maple Leafs needed a stoppage-time goal from Stephen Eustaquio to edge past South Africa in Los Angeles. Next up is Morocco in Houston.
contentParagraphs.zh: 世界杯，如果我们需要提醒的话，直到淘汰赛才真正开始。没有弥补休息日。没有第二次机会。加大强度。 Stale Solbakken 显然做对了——把你的先发球员留到第二轮。 推荐故事列表 4 项列表 4 项中的 1 项 梅西再次得分，但阿根廷队在世界杯上被佛得角惊吓 4 项中的 2 项 英格兰 vs 墨西哥 世界杯淘汰赛如期进行列表 4 项中的 3 项 埃及证实警察推动了球员，世界杯酒店总监 列表 4 项中的 4 项 萨拉赫等待着梅西，因为埃及在世界杯点球大战中击败了澳大利亚欧足联有7支球队从32强晋级，其中索尔巴肯的挪威队在第一组基本输给法国队后得到了充分的休息。三东道主加拿大、墨西哥和美国再次以三名身份进入中北美洲及加勒比海足球协会 16 强赛，这也是自 2014 年（当时是哥斯达黎加、墨西哥和美国）以来的第一次。南美国家有四个国家晋级：阿根廷、巴西、哥伦比亚和巴拉圭。巴拉圭的方法不像索尔巴肯那样——全力以赴从小组赛中挤进，然后通过快速逆转和跨国战斗在小组赛中与德国队相遇。马萨诸塞州福克斯伯勒。但阿尔比罗亚队还有足够的实力，成为第一支在世界杯点球大战中击败德国队的球队。法国队的四强——奥斯曼·登贝莱、德西雷·杜埃、基利安·姆巴佩和迈克尔·奥利塞——在小组赛中证明了所向披靡。此外，布拉德利·巴克拉取代杜埃，在32强赛中以3-0战胜瑞典，加快了步伐。法国队唯一的问题可能是攻击手过多。教练迪迪埃·德尚能让包括拉扬·切尔基在内的所有人都高兴吗？欧洲人制定标准比利时、英格兰、葡萄牙、西班牙和瑞士与法国和挪威一起成为32强赛的获胜者。比利时和英格兰必须与非洲对手对抗。比利时队以 2-0 落后塞内加尔队，之后尤里·蒂莱曼斯和罗梅卢·卢卡库将比赛拖入加时赛，蒂莱曼斯的点球将比分定为 3-2。英格兰队在哈里·凯恩的进球以 2-1 获胜之前受到刚果民主共和国的考验。葡萄牙队以 2-1 击败克罗地亚队，因为我们了解到世界杯球中的一个缺口可以记录一根头发丝的宽度触球，VAR 审查曾使用这一点来取消补时15分多钟，克罗地亚队扳平比分。世界杯传统强队的灭亡继意大利（未能晋级）和乌拉圭（小组赛后出局）之后，德国队也跻身世界杯衰退强队之列。直到最近，德国队似乎已经准备好挑战第五个冠军头衔。 38 岁的朱利安·纳格尔斯曼 (Julian Nagelsmann) 和以 40 岁门将曼努埃尔·诺伊尔 (Manuel Neuer) 为首的老将和年轻人看起来将引领一场复兴。相反，德国在点球大战中令人震惊地输给了巴拉圭，这是世界杯上的第一次，也是德国自 1976 年欧洲杯以来第一次点球失利。乔纳森·塔赫的头球本可以做到这一点，但 VAR 审查发现了门将干扰。“这是连续第三次被淘汰，所以我们不再是一流球队的一部分，”纳格尔斯曼说，他已经离开并可能由尤尔根取代。克洛普。非洲人留下痕迹与前冠军西班牙和乌拉圭战平？这只是终极弱旅佛得角的一场热身赛，在第 111 分钟迪尼·博尔赫斯的乌龙球让阿根廷进入加时赛，最终以 3-2 落败。虽然阿尔及利亚、科特迪瓦、刚果民主共和国、加纳、塞内加尔和南非都在 32 强赛中出局，但他们让这场比赛值得一看。也许玩一点愤世嫉俗的比赛会有所帮助。或者一些不妥协的防守，结果对埃及和摩洛哥起了作用，他们分别淘汰了澳大利亚和荷兰。尽管人们都在谈论穆罕默德·瓦赫比领导下的进攻，但阿特拉斯雄狮队到目前为止仍然坚持他们的 2022 年防守枪。伊萨·迪奥普伤停补时阶段的射门和亚辛·布努的点球阻击帮助他们战胜了荷兰队。金靴奖竞赛莱昂内尔·梅西的手术般的触感让他为阿根廷队对阵佛得角的比赛打进了首开纪录，将他的进球总数增加到了七个。同时，在对阵挪威队的比赛中打进创造者球后，基利安·姆巴佩在对阵瑞典队的比赛中梅开二度，重返进球模式。这使得姆巴佩，寻求成为第一个背靠背金靴奖得主，获得了六个进球。挪威的埃尔林·哈兰德和英格兰的哈里·凯恩在晚些时候保存了他们的进球——他们各有五个进球。梅西也以 20 个进球位居历史榜首，比姆巴佩多两个。主场优势绝对很重要，至少对墨西哥和美国来说是这样。爱尔兰队提高了自 1970 年以来的世界杯不败纪录。阿兹台克球场即将进入与英格兰队的最后 16 场比赛。墨西哥队也创下了360分钟不胜纪录，自1986年以来首次进入“第五场比赛”，但扩大的参赛阵容意味着距离四分之一决赛还差一场。缺点：墨西哥至少有四人因庆祝活动而死亡。在比赛开始前，有人担心美国观众会兴奋不已。在加利福尼亚州圣克拉拉以 2-0 战胜波斯尼亚和黑塞哥维那后，这些记录已被删除。不过，结果可能代价高昂，因为弗拉林·巴洛贡（Folarin Balogun）将在经过VAR审查后收到红牌后停赛。 与此同时，加拿大成为第一个在加利福尼亚州和德克萨斯州采取行动的联合主办国。枫叶队需要斯蒂芬·尤斯塔奎奥在补时阶段的进球才能在洛杉矶击败南非队。接下来是休斯敦的摩洛哥。

articles.title: 3,000% bonuses but a growing wealth divide: South Korea grapples with its AI chip boom
articles.summary:
Powered by chipmakers Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix, South Korea is seeing a surge in wealth, but there are questions over who gets to share in the profits
When South Korea’s most high-profile divorce case returned to court last month, the lawyers were arguing not just about t
articles.titleZh: 3,000% 奖金，但贫富差距日益扩大：韩国努力应对 AI 芯片热潮
articles.summaryZh:
在芯片制造商三星电子和 SK 海力士的推动下，韩国的财富正在激增，但谁能分享利润却存在疑问
上个月，当韩国最受瞩目的离婚案返回法庭时，律师们争论的不仅仅是离婚问题。

contentParagraphs.en: Powered by chipmakers Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix, South Korea is seeing a surge in wealth, but there are questions over who gets to share in the profitsWhen South Korea’s most high-profile divorce case returned to court last month, the lawyers were arguing not just about the breakdown of a relationship, but also the exact date at which to value shares in one specific company.The judges’ decision in Seoul could change the value of business tycoon Chey Tae-won’s assets by billions of dollars. The shares were in the holding company behind SK Hynix, the manufacturer of chips powering AI systems around the world.South Korea is one of the world’s biggest makers of these high-value chips and the country is seeing an unprecedented wealth boom from their rise. From workers in tech firms collecting six-figure bonuses, to ordinary people seeing massive investment returns – all of it driving a surge in luxury spending.But only a small slice of the population is cashing in, sparking a wider debate about who should have a share in the profits of the country’s most valuable industry. As South Korea grapples with widening inequality, calls are growing for some of the earnings – or the taxes they generate – to be spread more widely.The huge wealth surge has been driven by two companies – Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix. The pair dominate the global supply of high-bandwidth memory, the specialised chips that AI systems need to run. Analysts project their combined operating profits could rise almost sevenfold this year.Their success has pushed South Korea’s main stock index – the Kospi – to record highs.The chipmakers have begun sharing those record profits with their staff on a scale the country has never seen. At Samsung, a memory-chip worker on a base salary of 80m won ($51,300) could receive bonuses close to 600m won ($384,900) this year, most of it in stock. That is roughly 17 times the average annual salary at a small South Korean firm.SK Hynix paid its workers a bonus of nearly 3,000% of their monthly salary earlier this year. Based on forecast profits, next year’s payout is projected to be several times larger.The signs of this flourishing wealth are dotted across the country. In satellite cities built around the chip factories south of Seoul luxury sales are surging.Workers celebrate as a display shows South Korea’s KOSPI index at an all-time high closing price of 9,063.84. Photograph: Jintak Han/ZUMA Press Wire/ShutterstockIn the first weeks of May, jewellery sales at one department store jumped 146%, while watch sales rose 85%. In Icheon, where SK Hynix has its main campus, imported car registrations surged 108% in February. Apartment prices near semiconductor company bus routes are rising at four times the wider Seoul average.Not everyone benefiting from the AI boom works in a chip factory.After watching financial videos online a few years ago, Brian Lee, a retiree in Seoul bought small amounts of SK Hynix and Samsung shares – and then forgot about them. His SK Hynix return is now 1,264%.“This is the result of my hard work, plus luck,” he says. “I feel guilty, and at the same time, even though I have yet to cash it out, I tend to spend more,” he said, adding he has started looking at collector watches.Who owns the profits?The explosion in wealth has driven questions about who is entitled to a share in the profits – and how to spread the wealth more evenly across society.“Over the years, the semiconductor industry benefited enormously from government support,” says Kim Yong-jin, professor of business administration at Sogang University in Seoul, pointing to decades of state investment in research and industrial policy. “So they have to think about society itself.”The president’s chief policy adviser was drawn into the argument in May, floating what he called a “citizen dividend”, arguing the wealth rested on foundations built by all Koreans over half a century.South Korean president Lee Jae-myung holds hands with Samsung Electronics Chairman Lee Jae-yong, right, and SK Group Chairman Chey Tae-won as they announced a new computer chipmaking hub on Monday. Photograph: Kim Min-Hee/Pool KYODONEWS/APThough some critics saw it as a plan to hand out cash or seize company profits outright, he later framed it more narrowly as a way to channel surplus tax revenues back to the public through structured investment.Opposition politicians called his comments akin to communism and the presidential office distanced itself from the plan.The question of shared gains is not only political. Samsung’s largest union nearly brought production to a halt in May, demanding a guaranteed share of profits, before a last-minute deal averted a strike. But the deal upset some in the company, as those in the phone and appliance division were set to receive only a fraction of what those working in chip making did.South Korea has long grappled with inequality. It has one of the highest rates of elderly poverty in the developed world, while rising housing and living costs have deepened pressure on many households.More Koreans feel their living standards have worsened than improved, despite the wealth piling up elsewhere. Manufacturing employment has fallen year on year for nearly two years. Nearly a million small businesses closed in 2025, with many owners left carrying huge debts. The income gap between the richest and poorest households hit a six-year high.“Everyone is talking about the boom, but most Koreans can’t feel it,” says Kyusuk Cho, a graduate student in information studies. “Life is getting more expensive and jobs are harder to find.”Strip out the two chipmakers – who make up more than 50% of the Kospi index – and the rest of the economy is barely moving.Kim Yong-jin says the gains should be shared between those who invested, those who worked, and the society that made it possible, in a way that strengthens the country over the long run, but that South Korea has yet to build a framework for how.“We need a consen

articles.title: But first, coffee: The drink that energized the American Revolution
articles.summary: Colonial Americans were drinking coffee long before they dumped tea into Boston Harbor or fought a war for independence. The establishments that served it were already brewing revolutionary ideas.
articles.titleZh: 但首先，咖啡：为美国革命注入活力的饮料
articles.summaryZh: 美国殖民地早在将茶叶倒入波士顿港或为独立而战之前就已经开始喝咖啡了。为其服务的机构已经在酝酿革命性的想法。

contentParagraphs.en: An illustration of the Boston Tea Party, when colonists dumped British East India Company tea into the harbor on Dec. 16, 1773. Some accounts say this marked a pivotal moment when Americans started loving coffee. But one historian says Americans were drinking lots of coffee before then.
contentParagraphs.zh: 波士顿茶党的插图， 1773年12月16日，殖民者将英国东印度公司的茶叶倾倒在港口。一些报道称，这标志着美国人开始喜爱咖啡的关键时刻。但一位历史学家说，在那之前，美国人喝了很多咖啡。

contentParagraphs.en: A consequential act of defiance secured tea's place as perhaps the most iconic beverage of America's colonial era.
contentParagraphs.zh: 随之而来的蔑视行为确保了茶叶作为美国殖民时代最具标志性的饮料的地位。

contentParagraphs.en: The Boston Tea Party became an essential ingredient in the recipe for revolution in the following years.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在接下来的几年里，波士顿茶党成为革命配方的重要组成部分。

contentParagraphs.en: But tea wasn't the only hot beverage with a prominent role in America's fight for independence.
contentParagraphs.zh: 但茶并不是唯一在美国争取独立的斗争中发挥重要作用的热饮。

contentParagraphs.en: Coffee was an important part of American culture from the start. And coffeehouses were essential, too — serving as hubs for brewing ideas of independence.
contentParagraphs.zh: 咖啡从一开始就是美国文化的重要组成部分。咖啡馆也是必不可少的—作为酝酿独立思想的中心。

contentParagraphs.en: As the United States celebrates 250 years, here's what to know about America's early history of coffee.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在美国庆祝250周年之际，以下是有关美国早期咖啡历史的知识。

contentParagraphs.en: Colonists were drinking coffee long before the United States existed
contentParagraphs.zh: 殖民者在美国存在很久以前就开始喝咖啡了

contentParagraphs.en: Europeans brought coffee with them when they came to America.
contentParagraphs.zh: 当欧洲人来到美国时，他们随身携带咖啡。

contentParagraphs.en: "The first documented example of a mortar and pestle used to grind coffee beans was on the Mayflower" in 1620, says historian Michelle Craig McDonald, the author of Coffee Nation: How One Commodity Transformed the Early United States.
contentParagraphs.zh: 历史学家米歇尔·克雷格·麦克唐纳（ Michelle Craig McDonald ）在《咖啡国家：一种商品如何改变美国早期》（ Coffee Nation: How One Commodity Transformed the Early United States ）一书中说： “第一个记录在案的用于研磨咖啡豆的砂浆和杵的例子是在1620年的五月花号上。”

contentParagraphs.en: "The fact that coffee was present so early is not surprising if you think about it," McDonald says. "A number of those who were on the Mayflower came to North America from Amsterdam, which was a major coffee trading center in Western Europe by the 17th century."
contentParagraphs.zh: “如果你仔细想想，咖啡这么早就出现的事实并不奇怪，”麦当劳说。“五月花号上的许多人从阿姆斯特丹来到北美，阿姆斯特丹在17世纪是西欧的主要咖啡贸易中心。”

contentParagraphs.en: The first coffeehouse in the colonies opened in 1676 in Boston, a century before the U.S. declared independence, she says. Some taverns sold coffee even earlier.
contentParagraphs.zh: 她说，殖民地的第一家咖啡馆于1676年在波士顿开业，比美国宣布独立早了一个世纪。一些酒馆甚至更早地出售咖啡。

contentParagraphs.en: The Boston Tea Party probably wasn't the dramatic turning point toward coffee that some claim
contentParagraphs.zh: 波士顿茶党可能不是一些人声称的咖啡的戏剧性转折点

contentParagraphs.en: On the night of Dec. 16, 1773, disgruntled colonists boarded three ships moored in Boston Harbor and threw overboard more than 92,000 pounds of tea owned by the British East India Company.
contentParagraphs.zh: 1773年12月16日晚上，心怀不满的殖民者登上三艘停泊在波士顿港的船只，将英国东印度公司拥有的92,000多磅茶叶扔到海里。

contentParagraphs.en: Tensions had been building between the Crown and the colonies over the previous decade, as Britain tried to levy taxes on its colonies to recoup war debts.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在过去的十年中，英国王室和殖民地之间的紧张关系一直在加剧，因为英国试图对其殖民地征税以收回战争债务。

contentParagraphs.en: The Boston Tea Party protest was targeted at the British government's passing of the Tea Act in 1773, which granted the East India Company a monopoly over tea sales in the colonies. While the British had removed some unpopular taxes in the preceding years, they left tea taxes in place. Colonial merchants were especially upset that the act allowed the East India Company to undercut their tea business.
contentParagraphs.zh: 波士顿茶党的抗议活动针对的是英国政府在1773年通过的《茶叶法案》，该法案赋予了东印度公司对殖民地茶叶销售的垄断权。虽然英国在前几年取消了一些不受欢迎的税收，但他们保留了茶叶税。殖民地商人对该法案允许东印度公司削弱他们的茶叶业务感到特别不安。

contentParagraphs.en: To build solidarity for their cause of sovereignty, some patriots called on colonialists to swear off tea in favor of coffee. It's why many histories point to the Boston Tea Party as a turning point when Americans switched from mostly drinking tea to mostly coffee. The anti-tea sentiment was immortalized in a founding father's now-famous letter.
contentParagraphs.zh: 为了为他们的主权事业建立团结，一些爱国者呼吁殖民主义者停止喝茶，转而喝咖啡。这就是为什么许多历史都指出波士顿茶党是美国人从主要喝茶转向主要喝咖啡的转折点。反茶情绪在一位开国元勋如今出名的信中永垂不朽。

contentParagraphs.en: In July 1774, John Adams (before he became the second U.S. president) wrote to his wife Abigail, recounting an incident during his travels. After a long day, he asked the proprietor of the house where he was lodging for a cup of tea, provided it was smuggled and free of British taxes.
contentParagraphs.zh: 1774年7月，约翰·亚当斯（在他成为美国第二任总统之前）写信给他的妻子阿比盖尔，讲述了他在旅行期间发生的事件。经过漫长的一天，他问房子的业主在哪里住一杯茶，前提是茶是走私的，而且没有英国税收。

contentParagraphs.en: " 'No sir, said she, we have renounced all Tea in this Place. I cant make Tea, but I'le make you Coffee.' Accordingly I have drank Coffee every Afternoon since, and have borne it very well. Tea must be universally renounced. I must be weaned, and the sooner, the better," Adams wrote.
contentParagraphs.zh: “不，先生，她说，我们已经放弃了这个地方的所有茶。我不能泡茶，但我会给你做咖啡。“因此，从那以后，我每天下午都喝咖啡，而且喝得很好。必须全面放弃茶叶。我必须断奶，越快越好， ”亚当斯写道。

contentParagraphs.en: Despite John Adams claiming a newfound patriotic duty to appreciate coffee, McDonald says colonists had been drinking lots of coffee all along.
contentParagraphs.zh: 尽管约翰·亚当斯（ John Adams ）声称自己有欣赏咖啡的新发现的爱国责任，但麦克唐纳说，殖民者一直喝很多咖啡。

contentParagraphs.en: She studied advertisements from the 1760s and '70s to estimate how many shops sold coffee versus tea. Even before the Boston Tea Party, she says, "coffee is definitely more broadly available than tea is."
contentParagraphs.zh: 她研究了1760年代和70年代的广告，以估计有多少商店销售咖啡和茶。她说，甚至在波士顿茶会之前， “咖啡肯定比茶更广泛。”

contentParagraphs.en: A big reason? It was cheaper. "Its price again per pound is significantly less, which tells you about its availability, its accessibility to drinkers."
contentParagraphs.zh: 一个很大的原因？它更便宜。“它的每磅价格又要低得多，这告诉你它的可用性，它对饮酒者的可及性。”

contentParagraphs.en: Historians say it's hard to definitively compare tea with coffee consumption, though, as official records from before America gained independence were inconsistent.
contentParagraphs.zh: 不过，历史学家表示，很难将茶叶与咖啡消费量进行确切的比较，因为美国获得独立之前的官方记录并不一致。

contentParagraphs.en: And smuggling was rampant, making official records even less reliable.
contentParagraphs.zh: 走私猖獗，官方记录更加不可靠。

contentParagraphs.en: "There is a vast amount of smuggling," says Joyce Chaplin, a professor of early American history at Harvard University. "So they're not paying formal duties on tea that they get from the Dutch. They're probably not paying formal duties on coffee from the French Caribbean."
contentParagraphs.zh: 哈佛大学早期美国历史教授乔伊斯·卓别林（ Joyce Chaplin ）说： “走私数量巨大。” “所以他们没有为他们从荷兰人那里得到的茶支付正式关税。他们可能没有为来自法属加勒比地区的咖啡支付正式关税。”

contentParagraphs.en: And Chaplin notes that people who loudly proclaimed a new appreciation for coffee over tea weren't always doing what they said. It could have been political pandering. "I do not drink tea that comes via the East India Company," she posits someone of the era saying. "But, you know, other sources are fine. Ditto for the coffee."
contentParagraphs.zh: 卓别林指出，那些大声宣称咖啡胜过茶的人并不总是按照他们所说的去做。这可能是政治上的迎合。“我不喝来自东印度公司的茶，”她假设那个时代的人说。“但是，你知道，其他来源都很好。咖啡也一样。”

contentParagraphs.en: Coffeehouses were a hub for revolutionary ideas
contentParagraphs.zh: 咖啡馆是革命思想的中心

contentParagraphs.en: A coffeepot with cover, circa 1795. It has an American eagle motif, made in China for the American market. Coffee was part of a growing trend of globalization in the colonial era.
contentParagraphs.zh: 带盖的咖啡壶，约1795年。它有一个美国鹰图案，在中国为美国市场制造。咖啡是殖民时代日益增长的全球化趋势的一部分。

contentParagraphs.en: Heritage Art/Heritage Images via Getty Images
contentParagraphs.zh: Getty Images的遗产艺术/遗产图片

contentParagraphs.en: Heritage Art/Heritage Images via Getty Images
contentParagraphs.zh: Getty Images的遗产艺术/遗产图片

contentParagraphs.en: In the colonial era, coffeehouses were hotbeds for seditious thought — where people planned acts of revolution.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在殖民时代，咖啡馆是煽动性思想的温床，人们在那里策划革命行动。

contentParagraphs.en: "Coffeehouses are kind of famous for being places where people think and plot things," says Mark Pendergrast, author of Uncommon Grounds: The History of Coffee and How It Transformed Our World.
contentParagraphs.zh: 《Uncommon Grounds: The History of Coffee and How It Transformed Our World》一书的作者马克·彭德格拉斯特（ Mark Pendergrast ）说： “咖啡馆以人们思考和策划事物的地方而闻名。

contentParagraphs.en: A coffeehouse called the Green Dragon served as one of the locations for planning the Boston Tea Party. Years earlier, the Old London Coffeehouse in Philadelphia was a meeting place for strategizing responses to another British tax, the Stamp Act of 1765.
contentParagraphs.zh: 一家名为Green Dragon的咖啡馆是策划波士顿茶会的地点之一。多年前，费城的老伦敦咖啡馆是制定应对另一项英国税收（ 1765年印花税法案）的战略的聚会场所。

contentParagraphs.en: In Britain, coffeehouses were nicknamed "penny universities," Pendergrast says: "because for a penny you could go and learn a whole lot by sitting around in a coffeehouse and discussing everything." The same atti
contentParagraphs.zh: 在英国，咖啡馆被昵称为“便士大学” ， Pendergrast说： “因为只要一分钱，你就可以坐在咖啡馆里讨论一切，学到很多东西。“同样的atti
